projectName: DJZS Protocol — Adversarial Reasoning Auditor for AI Agents
description: DJZS is a provably-isolated adversarial reasoning audit for autonomous AI agents. It provides a hostile second brain that stress-tests agent logic inside a Phala TEE (Intel SGX enclave), then permanently certifies the result on-chain via Irys Datachain. Every audit produces a binary PASS/FAIL verdict with machine-parseable DJZS-LF failure codes. On-chain escrow gates block capital deployment when reasoning fails audit. Trust scores are written to Base Mainnet for composable reputation tracking.
conversationLog: See CONVERSATION_LOG.md in repository root — covers registration, strategy, sprint planning, and technical decisions across the full build process.
_demoVideoNote: User will record the demo video using DEMO_SCRIPT.md as the narration guide. Update this field with the hosted video URL before final API submission.

submissionMetadata.agentHarness: DJZS Adversarial Oracle (Venice AI + Phala TEE)

partnerIntegrations.venice: Inference backbone for adversarial audit engine
partnerIntegrations.base: ERC-8004 identity on Base Mainnet, USDC payment verification, contract deployment target
